item.title: Issue: Using Include causes the "Index was out of range" exception when trying to sort - @alexandis
item.description:
I did not have the bugs like this in EfCore 3.x. Now I have it in EfCore 5. I don't see what could be wrong. Here is the entity binding (Oracle is used as a DB):

        builder.Entity<FineDigitGroup>()
            .HasMany(x => x.FineDigitGroupLanguages)
            .WithOne()
            .HasPrincipalKey(x => new { x.TenantId, x.OrdinanceId, x.ClassId, x.GroupId })
            .HasForeignKey(x => new { x.TenantId, x.OrdinanceId, x.ClassId, x.GroupId });
        builder.Entity<FineDigitGroup>(b =>
        {
            b.ToTable("MD_CA_FND_GROUP", CoreDbProperties.DbSchema);
            b.ConfigureByConvention();
            b.HasKey(x => new { x.TenantId, x.OrdinanceId, x.ClassId, x.GroupId });
            b.Property(x => x.TenantId).HasColumnName("C_TENANT").IsRequired();
            b.Property(x => x.OrdinanceId).HasColumnName("C_ORDINANCE").IsRequired();
            b.Property(x => x.ClassId).HasColumnName("C_CLASS").IsRequired();
            b.Property(x => x.GroupId).HasColumnName("C_GROUP").IsRequired();
            b.Ignore(x => x.ShortDescription);
            b.MapLogProperties(); // Not relevant - binds some other fields
        });

        builder.Entity<FineDigitGroupLanguage>(b =>
        {
            b.ToTable("MD_CA_FND_GROUP_LAN", CoreDbProperties.DbSchema);
            b.ConfigureByConvention();
            b.HasKey(x => new { x.TenantId, x.OrdinanceId, x.ClassId, x.GroupId, x.LanguageCode });
            b.Property(x => x.TenantId).HasColumnName("C_TENANT").IsRequired();
            b.Property(x => x.OrdinanceId).HasColumnName("C_ORDINANCE").IsRequired();
            b.Property(x => x.ClassId).HasColumnName("C_CLASS").IsRequired();
            b.Property(x => x.GroupId).HasColumnName("C_GROUP").IsRequired();
            b.Property(x => x.LanguageCode)
                .HasConversion(new ValueConverter<string, string>(v => v.Trim(), v => v.Trim()))
                .HasColumnName("C_LANGUAGE").IsRequired().HasMaxLength(FineDigitGroupLanguageDbConsts.LanguageCodeMaxLength);
            b.Property(x => x.ShortDescription)
                .HasConversion(new ValueConverter<string, string>(v => v.Trim(), v => v.Trim()))
                .HasColumnName("S_DESC_INT").HasMaxLength(FineDigitGroupLanguageDbConsts.ShortDescriptionMaxLength);
            b.Property(x => x.FullDescription)
                .HasConversion(new ValueConverter<string, string>(v => v.Trim(), v => v.Trim()))
                .HasColumnName("S_DESC").HasMaxLength(FineDigitGroupLanguageDbConsts.FullDescriptionMaxLength);
            b.MapLogProperties(); // Not relevant - binds some other fields
        });

And the simplified method to get data list:

    public async Task<List<FineDigitGroup>> GetListAsync
    (
        int tenantId,
        int? ordinanceId = null,
        int? classId = null,
        int? groupId = null,
        string shortDescription = null,
        string sorting = null,
        int maxResultCount = int.MaxValue,
        int skipCount = 0,
        string userContentLangRestriction = null,
        CancellationToken cancellationToken = default
    )
    {
        var query = (await GetDbSetAsync()).Include(x => x.FineDigitGroupLanguages);

        var sortingExpression = string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(sorting) ? FineDigitGroupDbConsts.DefaultSorting : sorting;

        return await query.OrderBy(sortingExpression).PageBy(skipCount, maxResultCount).ToListAsync(cancellationToken);
    }

When I try to sort by "classId" in my grid - I am getting the exception:

[15:01:13 ERR] Index was out of range. Must be non-negative and less than the size of the collection. (Parameter 'index') System.ArgumentOutOfRangeException: Index was out of range. Must be non-negative and less than the size of the collection. (Parameter 'index') at System.Collections.Generic.List1.get_Item(Int32 index) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.SqlExpressions.SelectExpression.ApplyCollectionJoin(Int32 collectionIndex, Int32 collectionId, Expression innerShaper, INavigationBase navigation, Type elementType, Boolean splitQuery) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.Internal.CollectionJoinApplyingExpressionVisitor.VisitExtension(Expression extensionExpression) at System.Linq.Expressions.Expression.Accept(ExpressionVisitor visitor) at System.Linq.Expressions.ExpressionVisitor.Visit(Expression node) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.IncludeExpression.VisitChildren(ExpressionVisitor visitor) at System.Linq.Expressions.ExpressionVisitor.VisitExtension(Expression node) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.Internal.CollectionJoinApplyingExpressionVisitor.VisitExtension(Expression extensionExpression) at System.Linq.Expressions.Expression.Accept(ExpressionVisitor visitor) at System.Linq.Expressions.ExpressionVisitor.Visit(Expression node) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.Internal.CollectionJoinApplyingExpressionVisitor.VisitExtension(Expression extensionExpression) at System.Linq.Expressions.Expression.Accept(ExpressionVisitor visitor) at System.Linq.Expressions.ExpressionVisitor.Visit(Expression node) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.RelationalQueryTranslationPostprocessor.Process(Expression query) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.QueryCompilationContext.CreateQueryExecutor[TResult](Expression query) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Storage.Database.CompileQuery[TResult](Expression query, Boolean async) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.Internal.QueryCompiler.CompileQueryCore[TResult](IDatabase database, Expression query, IModel model, Boolean async) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.Internal.QueryCompiler.<>c__DisplayClass12_01.b__0() at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.Internal.CompiledQueryCache.GetOrAddQuery[TResult](Object cacheKey, Func1 compiler) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.Internal.QueryCompiler.ExecuteAsync[TResult](Expression query, CancellationToken cancellationToken) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.Internal.EntityQueryProvider.ExecuteAsync[TResult](Expression expression, CancellationToken cancellationToken) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.Internal.EntityQueryable1.GetAsyncEnumerator(CancellationToken cancellationToken) at System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ConfiguredCancelableAsyncEnumerable1.GetAsyncEnumerator() at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.EntityFrameworkQueryableExtensions.ToListAsync[TSource](IQueryable1 source, CancellationToken cancellationToken) at AbxEps.Fines.FineDigitGroups.EfCoreFineDigitGroupRepository.GetListAsync(Int32 tenantId, Nullable1 ordinanceId, Nullable1 classId, Nullable`1 groupId, String shortDescription, String sorting, Int32 maxResultCount, Int32 skipCount, String userContentLangRestriction, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\CT\AbxEps.Fines\aspnet-core\src\AbxEps.Fines.EntityFrameworkCore\FineDigitGroups\EfCoreFineDigitGroupRepository.cs:line 57

When I remove Include(x => x.FineDigitGroupLanguages) part - everything is fine.

Emphasizing - there is no issue with binding and getting FineDigitGroupLanguages data itself: it works fine as well. The problem begins when I try to sort or filter this FineDigitGroup list.

item.title: Issue: Check for arithmetic overflow and SELECT with (int)? Max throws KeyNotFoundException - @GraySerg
item.description:
File a bug

Check option "Check for arithmetic overflow" on project

Include your code

    public class LogContext : DbContext
    {
        public LogContext(DbContextOptions<LogContext> options) : base(options)
        {
        }
        public DbSet<AccountLog> AccountLogs { get; set; }

        protected override void OnModelCreating(ModelBuilder modelBuilder)
        {
            base.OnModelCreating(modelBuilder);
            modelBuilder.ApplyConfiguration(new AccountLogConfiguration());
        }
    }
    [Table("AccountLogs")]
    public class AccountLog
    {
        public AccountLog()
        {

        }
        [Key, DatabaseGenerated(DatabaseGeneratedOption.None)]
        public int AccountLogId { get; }
        public string Text { get; set; }
        public DateTime Time { get; }
    }
    internal class AccountLogConfiguration : IEntityTypeConfiguration<AccountLog>
    {

        public void Configure(EntityTypeBuilder<AccountLog> builder)
        {
            builder.Property(x => x.AccountLogId);
            builder.Property(x => x.Text);
            builder.Property(x => x.Time);
        }
    }
    class Program
    {
        static async Task Main(string[] args)
        {
            var finoptions = new Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContextOptionsBuilder<LogContext>().UseSqlServer("server=localhost;Integrated Security=SSPI;MultipleActiveResultSets=true;Initial Catalog=MyDb", options => options.EnableRetryOnFailure());
            LogContext ct = new LogContext(finoptions.Options);
            int maxCode = ct.AccountLogs.Select(x => x.AccountLogId).Max();//Works
            int maxCodeFailed = ct.AccountLogs.Select(x => (int?)x.AccountLogId).Max().GetValueOrDefault(0);//Exception here
}
}

Include stack traces

at System.ThrowHelper.ThrowKeyNotFoundException() at System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary2.get_Item(TKey key) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.RelationalQueryableMethodTranslatingExpressionVisitor.TranslateMax(ShapedQueryExpression source, LambdaExpression selector, Type resultType) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.QueryableMethodTranslatingExpressionVisitor.VisitMethodCall(MethodCallExpression methodCallExpression) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.QueryCompilationContext.CreateQueryExecutor[TResult](Expression query) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.Internal.QueryCompiler.<>c__DisplayClass9_01.b__0() at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.Internal.CompiledQueryCache.GetOrAddQueryCore[TFunc](Object cacheKey, Func`1 compiler) at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.Internal.QueryCompiler.Execute[TResult](Expression query) at ConsoleApp8.Program.

d__0.MoveNext() in E:\Projects\ConsoleApp8\ConsoleApp8\Program.cs:line 72

Include provider and version information

EF Core version: 3.1.17 Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer Target framework: .NET Framework 4.8 Operating system: Windows 10 Pro IDE: (e.g. Visual Studio 2019 16.10.3)

item.title: Issue: Is there a missing statement in EFCore documentation about Lazy Loading? - @Paolo-N
item.description:
I enabled Lazy Loading with proxies and the documentation here says "Lazy loading means that the related data is transparently loaded from the database when the navigation property is accessed."

I was struggling with a navigation property update trying to set the navigation property to null. It didn't work because apparently EFcore loads the Entity from the database after null assignment (probably never and it doesn't detect changes) and I had to use eager loading to made it work (I have to thank this stackoverflow post).

I know I can use Remove method, but it's not what I want because I created a custom entity validator to use before SaveChanges and I want that property to be null in some cases to fail the validation.

The documentation of EF6 about lazy loading says "Lazy loading is the process whereby an entity or collection of entities is automatically loaded from the database the first time that a property referring to the entity/entities is accessed." and I think is clearer then EFCore one.

So my questions are:

  • Do you think the doc should be clearer?
  • Did I miss something in the doc?

Thanks

item.title: Issue: Blazor concurrently updating objects - @mkalinski93
item.description:
Hello,

I have a question about updating objects within Blazor WebAssembly (ASP.Net Core Hosted). In my Application, I have an updater which is a background job (Hangfire). This job gets tons of data and I need to update my objects in a transaction. Unfortunately, when I go into my blazor application and wants to update a specific object (in my example adding an Asp Net Core Identity Role user), I will get the following error: "An exception has been raised that is likely due to a transient failure. Consider enabling transient error resiliency by adding 'EnableRetryOnFailure()' to the 'UseMySql' call."

 An exception has been raised that is likely due to a transient failure. Consider enabling transient error resiliency by adding 'EnableRetryOnFailure()' to the 'UseMySql' call.

         at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Infrastructure.ResourceInvoker.<InvokeNextResourceFilter>g__Awaited|24_0(ResourceInvoker invoker, Task lastTask, State next, Scope scope, Object state, Boolean isCompleted)
         at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Infrastructure.ResourceInvoker.Rethrow(ResourceExecutedContextSealed context)
         at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Infrastructure.ResourceInvoker.Next(State& next, Scope& scope, Object& state, Boolean& isCompleted)
         at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Infrastructure.ResourceInvoker.<InvokeFilterPipelineAsync>g__Awaited|19_0(ResourceInvoker invoker, Task lastTask, State next, Scope scope, Object state, Boolean isCompleted)
         at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Infrastructure.ResourceInvoker.<InvokeAsync>g__Logged|17_1(ResourceInvoker invoker)
         at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Routing.EndpointMiddleware.<Invoke>g__AwaitRequestTask|6_0(Endpoint endpoint, Task requestTask, ILogger logger)
         at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.Extensions.MapMiddleware.Invoke(HttpContext context)
         at Swashbuckle.AspNetCore.SwaggerUI.SwaggerUIMiddleware.Invoke(HttpContext httpContext)
         at Swashbuckle.AspNetCore.Swagger.SwaggerMiddleware.Invoke(HttpContext httpContext, ISwaggerProvider swaggerProvider)
         at Pricing.Server.Common.Middlewares.ExceptionMiddleware.InvokeAsync(HttpContext context) in /Project/src/Server/Pricing.Server/Common/Middlewares/ExceptionMiddleware.cs:line 53

I´ve activate the EnableRetryOnFailure strategy, but this doesn´t cooperate with user-initiated transactions.

Somehow, when the background worker is doing its part, I am not able to update any other objects anymore. Also it seems that something is in transaction, the dbcontext is blocked for writing purposes. My services and the dbcontext is added as a scoped service.

Please let me know if you need additional information.

UnitOfWork.cs - Transactions

        private IDbContextTransaction _transaction;
        public async Task BeginTransactionAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) {
            if (_dbFactory.DbContext.Database.CurrentTransaction != null && _transaction != null)
                throw new TransactionException("There´s already an open transaction");
            _transaction = await _dbFactory.DbContext.Database.BeginTransactionAsync(cancellationToken);
        }

        public async Task CommitTransactionAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) {
            await _transaction.CommitAsync(cancellationToken);
        }

        public async Task RollbackTransactionAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) {
            await _transaction.RollbackAsync(cancellationToken);
            await _transaction.DisposeAsync();
            _transaction = null;
        }

services.AddDbContext<PricingDbContext>(options => {
                options.UseMySql(connectionString, ServerVersion.AutoDetect(connectionString), builder => {
                    builder.EnableStringComparisonTranslations();
                });
#if DEBUG
                options.EnableSensitiveDataLogging();
                options.EnableDetailedErrors();
#endif
            });
            services.AddScoped<DbContext, PricingDbContext>();
            services.AddIdentity<ApplicationUser, ApplicationRole>()
                .AddEntityFrameworkStores<PricingDbContext>()
                .AddDefaultTokenProviders();
            services.AddScoped<Func<PricingDbContext>>(provider => () => provider.GetService<PricingDbContext>());
            services.AddScoped<DbFactory>();
            services.AddScoped<IUnitOfWork, UnitOfWork>();

My assumption is that when I submit the job request to the server, the Server then will create a new background instance which my dbcontext gets scoped into. The very next request from my frontend should create a new instance of DbContext. But somehow the update call just gets stuck and the command times out.

*EDIT: It seems that only this method is not working.

IdentityResult addResult = await _userManager.AddToRoleAsync(applicationUser, role.Name);

Setup

Blazor WebAssembly EntityFrameworkCore 5.0.7 Net5.0

item.title: Issue: Cannot change the base class of ApplicationDbContext - @avinashtauro-arctechinfo
item.description:
ApplicationDbContext which is auto generated always has DbContext as the base class. We use Identity and need the base class to be IdentityDbContext<ApplicationUser, IdentityRole, string>

Currently our developers, copy-paste this base class after every reverse engineering task, and add the using's for these. Though it takes only a few seconds , would be good if there is an option in settings to specify base class instead of DbContext.

item.title: Issue: PossibleIncorrectRequiredNavigationWithQueryFilterInteractionWarning with table by hierarchy and navigations - @phija
item.description:
Hi all,

I am using EF Core 5.0.3 and have some nested entities with also Table per Hierarchy (TPH) in place. I also make use of the AspNet Boilerplate (ABP) and AspNetZero packages which provide global query filters for soft delete and multi tenancy functionality. (interfaces are IMayHaveTenant, IMustHaveTenant and ISoftDelete). I use only IMayHaveTenant at the moment. In detail the entities look like this:

public class Base : Entity<Guid>, IMayHaveTenant
{
    public int? TenantId { get; set; }
    ...
}

public class A : Base
{
    public B InnerPart { get; set; }
    ...
}

public class B : Entity<Guid>, IMayHaveTenant
{
    public int? TenantId { get; set; }
    ...
}

My DbContext and model configuration look like this:

public class MyDbContext : AbpZeroDbContext<Tenant, Role, User, Ptw100DbContext>, IAbpPersistedGrantDbContext
{
    public DbSet<A> As { get; set }
    public DbSet<B> Bs { get; set }
    ...

    protected override void OnModelCreating(ModelBuilder modelBuilder)
    {
        modelBuilder.Entity<A>(b =>
        {
            b.HasOne(p => p.InnerPart).WithOne().HasForeignKey<A>("InnerPartId");
        }
    }
}

I need that explicit navigation statement because otherwise eager loading is not working with tracking the entities. But when running the application I get the EF Core warning:

.. [WRN] { Id: 10622, Name: "Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Model.Validation.PossibleIncorrectRequiredNavigationWithQueryFilterInteractionWarning" } Entity '"B"' has a global query filter defined and is the required end of a relationship wit
h the entity '"A"'. This may lead to unexpected results when the required entity is filtered out. Either configure the navigation as optional, or define matching query filters for both entities in the navigation. See https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=21
31316 for more information.

The point is: I don't want to make the navigation optional because it is not. If there is no B then there is something broken and A is not in a state how it should be.

I investigated by debugging a query to identify the global query filters to possibly match them on the parent entity type but the resulting query for a single query shows me it is basically just the IMayHaveTenant (?correct me if I'm wrong!) which is not seen as it maybe should be:

DECLARE @__ef_filter__p_2 bit = CAST(0 AS bit);
DECLARE @__ef_filter__CurrentTenantId_3 int = 1;
DECLARE @__ef_filter__p_0 bit = CAST(0 AS bit);
DECLARE @__ef_filter__CurrentTenantId_1 int = 1;
DECLARE @__id_0 uniqueIdentifier = 'b9ded491-8651-469d-8e53-08d94a9dde27';

SELECT [r].[Id], ..., [r].[TenantId], ..., [d].[Id], ..., [t].[Id], ..., [t].[TenantId]
FROM [Base] AS [r]
INNER JOIN [A] AS [d] ON [r].[Id] = [d].[Id]
INNER JOIN (
    SELECT [i].[Id], [i].[CreationTime], [i].[CreatorUserId], [i].[LastModificationTime], [i].[LastModifierUserId], [i].[SamplingTime], [i].[TenantId]
    FROM [B] AS [i]
    WHERE (@__ef_filter__p_2 = CAST(1 AS bit)) OR ([i].[TenantId] = @__ef_filter__CurrentTenantId_3)
) AS [t] ON [d].[SetupId] = [t].[Id]
WHERE ((@__ef_filter__p_0 = CAST(1 AS bit)) OR ([r].[TenantId] = @__ef_filter__CurrentTenantId_1)) AND ([r].[Id] = @__id_0)

Question

How can I make the warning disappear without making the navigation optional? Adding the global query filter to A is not possible because then I get an error that it needs to be added to the base class (don't remember the exact error here). Adding it to Base doesn't make sense because there it is already existing.

Thanks for any help!

EF Core version: 5.0.3 Database provider: Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer Target framework: .NET 5.0 Operating system: windows 10 IDE: independent

item.title: Issue: Sql Server built in TVF being escaped in 5.x - @pmiddleton
item.description:
@smitpatel

I am trying to use the built in TVF OPENJSON. In the 5.x branch the TVF call is being escaped by brackets [] and thus failed.
I tried the latest 6.x and this has been fixed.

Do you know of any way to make this work in 5.x? I couldn't think of any.

Oh the irony - the first time I try to use TVF at work and I find an edge case I missed in the original code :(

item.title: Issue: HasConversion<string>() always returns "False" - @mrlife
item.description:
File a bug

Here's the docs section that details the conversion options (bool is at the top).

Include your code

Model:

public class MyModel
{
    public bool IsTrue { get; set; }
}

Context:

modelBuilder
.Entity<MyModel>(eb =>
{
    eb.HasNoKey();
    eb.ToView("MyView");
    eb.Property(v => v.IsTrue).HasColumnName("yes_no_value").HasConversion<string>(); // this line always returns False
});

Changing the mapping/conversion line to the following works:

eb.Property(v => v.IsTrue).HasColumnName("yes_no_value").HasConversion(new BoolToStringConverter("N", "Y"));

Include provider and version information

EF Core version: 5.0.8 Database provider: Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer Target framework: .NET 5.0 Operating system: macOS 11.4 IDE: Visual Studio for Mac 8.10 build 10

item.title: Issue: Add-Migration crashes with NullReferenceException when changing the schema for Microsoft.Identity tables - @fededim
item.description:
Hi,

I am using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Identity.EntityFrameworkCore 5.0.8. If I try to customize in the DbContext the schema name for the AspNet* tables e.g.

item.title: Issue: Scaffold tool can create an inverse property with a similar name to a column property and get confused - @conficient
item.description:
If a table has a foreign key column and a value column with a similar name, then then scaffold tool can create a model which confuses the model builder as they have the same property name if case isn't matched.

Detail

I have a database with a CPSorder table with a column CPSChargePartner column (smallmoney, nullable).

This table also has a foreign key to a Partners table with the foreign key column called CPSchargePartnerID.

In earlier versions of the dotnet ef dbcontext scaffold command had no issues. Recently I upgraded my code to use EF Core tools 5.0.8 and rebuilt the context (full command below):

dotnet ef dbcontext scaffold "Server=xxx;Database=AnvilFGDEV;Trusted_Connection=True;" Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer --force  --namespace AnvilDB --context AnvilDBcontext --context-dir Context --data-annotations --use-database-names --output-dir Tables >_build_db.log

This generated everything without issues. However when I tried to use the context it crashed with a NullReferenceException somewhere in the create model process:

Message: 
    Test method AnvilDB.Tests.AnvilDBcontext_Table_Tests.AnvilFGDdev threw exception: 
    System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.

  Stack Trace: 
    InversePropertyAttributeConvention.ConfigureInverseNavigation(IConventionEntityTypeBuilder entityTypeBuilder, MemberInfo navigationMemberInfo, IConventionEntityTypeBuilder targetEntityTypeBuilder, InversePropertyAttribute attribute)
    InversePropertyAttributeConvention.Process(IConventionEntityTypeBuilder entityTypeBuilder, MemberInfo navigationMemberInfo, Type targetClrType, InversePropertyAttribute attribute)
    InversePropertyAttributeConvention.ProcessEntityTypeAdded(IConventionEntityTypeBuilder entityTypeBuilder, MemberInfo navigationMemberInfo, Type targetClrType, InversePropertyAttribute attribute, IConventionContext`1 context)
    NavigationAttributeConventionBase`1.ProcessEntityTypeAdded(IConventionEntityTypeBuilder entityTypeBuilder, IConventionContext`1 context)
    ImmediateConventionScope.OnEntityTypeAdded(IConventionEntityTypeBuilder entityTypeBuilder)
    OnEntityTypeAddedNode.Run(ConventionDispatcher dispatcher)
    DelayedConventionScope.Run(ConventionDispatcher dispatcher)
    ConventionBatch.Run()
    ConventionBatch.Dispose()
    ImmediateConventionScope.OnModelInitialized(IConventionModelBuilder modelBuilder)
    <26 more frames...>
    DbContext.get_InternalServiceProvider()
    DbContext.get_DbContextDependencies()
    DbContext.get_Model()
    InternalDbSet`1.get_EntityType()
    InternalDbSet`1.CheckState()
    InternalDbSet`1.get_EntityQueryable()
    IEnumerable<TEntity>.GetEnumerator()
    List`1.ctor(IEnumerable`1 collection)
    Enumerable.ToList[TSource](IEnumerable`1 source)
    AnvilDBcontext_Table_Tests.AnvilFGDdev() line 21

Cue a lot of headscratching and also referring to this issue. I then tried updating to EF Core 6 preview and trying to run the generated context in .NET 6 preview: this resulted in this:

 Message: 
    Test method TestContext.AnvilFGDEV_Test.ReadTablesAsync threw exception: 
    System.Collections.Generic.KeyNotFoundException: The given key 'System.Nullable`1[System.Decimal] CPSChargePartner' was not present in the dictionary.

  Stack Trace: 
    Immutable.dll:token 0x6000490+0x34
    EntityFrameworkCore.dll:token 0x6001591+0x4c

Cause

So the inverse property name should be CPSchargePartner but CPSChargePartner (the column) was being matched.

When the scaffold tool ran, it created the following in CPSOrder.cs:

        [Column(TypeName = "smallmoney")]
        public decimal? CPSChargePartner { get; set; }

        // other lines skipped

        [ForeignKey(nameof(CPSchargePartnerID))]
        [InverseProperty(nameof(Partner.CPSorders))]
        public virtual Partner CPSchargePartner { get; set; }

In the related table Partners.cs we have:

        [InverseProperty(nameof(CPSorder.CPSchargePartner))]
        public virtual ICollection<CPSorder> CPSorders { get; set; }

and in the context:

        entity.HasOne(d => d.CPSchargePartner)
            .WithMany(p => p.CPSorders)
            .HasForeignKey(d => d.CPSchargePartnerID)
            .HasConstraintName("FK_CPSorders_Partners");

So although everything looks okay, when the model is being created it tries to use the property CPSChargePartner which isn't a foreign key and so wasn't in the map. If the mapping/lookup isn't case sensitive perhaps the scaffold tool should refuse to create possible conflicting properties?

I was able to work around this by renaming the property so that it would not get picked up:

        [Column("CPSChargePartner", TypeName = "smallmoney")]
        public decimal? CPSChargeForPartner { get; set; }

item.title: Issue: Disposing DbContext scoped to a Razor component lifetime throws InvalidOperationException on quick refreshes - @amal-stack
item.description:
I'm trying to scope a DB Context to the lifetime of a Razor component that needs to execute a lot of queries throughout its lifetime. I found this as a better alternative to creating a new DB Context per operation and I am following the instructions at Microsoft Docs.

Context

I have a DbContext which is created in OnInitializedAsync() of a Razor component using the injected IDbContextFactory<AppDbContext> and scoped to the lifetime of a Razor component. For this, the Razor component implements IDisposable and hence has a Dispose method. There is a complex EF Core query being executed in OnInitializedAsync. A flag isDbContextBusy indicates if the DB context is busy.

Code

@inject IDbContextFactory<AppDbContext>
@implements IDisposable

...
@code {
    private AppDbContext dbContext;
    private bool isDbContextBusy;

    protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync()
    {
        dbContext = DbContextFactory.CreateDbContext();
        isDbContextBusy = true;
        try { var entites = await dbContext.Entites.AsSplitQuery().{Any Complex Query Here }; }
        finally { isDbContextBusy = false; }
    }
    
    public void Dispose() => dbContext?.Dispose();
}

Problem

This works as expected. However, when I keep hitting refresh multiple times quickly in succession(as a minor stress test), the dbContext?.Dispose() in the component's Dispose() method ends up throwing an InvalidOperationException exception:

System.InvalidOperationException: 'Can't close, connection is in state Connecting'

I suppose refreshing the page ends the lifetime of the component which then attempts to call Dispose() before the DbContext is initialized.

How do I exactly handle this exception or is there any way to prevent it in the first place?

Stack Trace

System.InvalidOperationException
  HResult=0x80131509
  Message=Can't close, connection is in state Connecting
  Source=Npgsql
  StackTrace:
   at Npgsql.NpgsqlConnection.Close(Boolean async, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Npgsql.NpgsqlConnection.Close()
   at Npgsql.NpgsqlConnection.Dispose(Boolean disposing)
   at System.ComponentModel.Component.Dispose()
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Storage.RelationalConnection.Dispose()
   at Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.ServiceLookup.ServiceProviderEngineScope.Dispose()
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext.Dispose()
   at KSD.ModelCollege.LearningAssessmentSystem.Areas.Users.Views.Accounts.InputStudentInfo.Dispose() in {Project Directory}\Areas\Users\Views\Accounts\InputStudentInfo.razor:line 247
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Rendering.ComponentState.Dispose()
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.RenderTree.Renderer.Dispose(Boolean disposing)

Provider and version information

EF Core version: 5.0.6 Database provider: Npgsql (5.0.5) Target framework: .NET 5.0 Operating system: Microsoft Windows Version 21H1 (OS Build 19043.1110) IDE: Visual Studio 2019 16.10.3

item.title: Issue: Multiple Outgoing FK From Entity To Itself: Incoming Inverse Nav Props Not Loaded Correctly Via String Inclusion - @jwbats
item.description:
File a bug

I have a Comment entity. I want my comments to be nested, so I'm giving it a ParentComment property. Every comment can point to the comment above it. But I'm also thinking about my performance: I want to retrieve all child comments (both direct and indirect) when retrieving top level comments (who have ParentComment == null). Therefore, every comment can also point to its TopComment, if it is not a top level comment itself.

My inverse properties DirectChildComments and AllChildComments, are not being filled out correctly when I load them via string inclusion.

Include your code

Entity

// regular props
...
public string Body { get; set; } = "";
...

// outgoing foreign keys
[ForeignKey("ParentCommentID")]
[InverseProperty("DirectChildComments")]
public Comment ParentComment { get; set; } = null;
public int? ParentCommentID { get; set; } = null;

[ForeignKey("TopCommentID")]
[InverseProperty("AllChildComments")]
public Comment TopComment { get; set; } = null;
public int? TopCommentID { get; set; } = null;

// incoming foreign keys
public List<Comment> DirectChildComments { get; set; } = new List<Comment>();
public List<Comment> AllChildComments { get; set; } = new List<Comment>();

Table

GO

(
	[ID] ASC
)WITH (PAD_INDEX = OFF, STATISTICS_NORECOMPUTE = OFF, IGNORE_DUP_KEY = OFF, ALLOW_ROW_LOCKS = ON, ALLOW_PAGE_LOCKS = ON, OPTIMIZE_FOR_SEQUENTIAL_KEY = OFF) ON [PRIMARY]
) ON [PRIMARY] TEXTIMAGE_ON [PRIMARY]

REFERENCES [dbo].[Account] ([ID])

REFERENCES [dbo].[Package] ([ID])

GO

Test Data

10	2	0	I'd love to own this package, as well.	0	0	2021-06-10 16:13:34.0000000	2021-06-10 16:13:34.0000000	58	4	205	4

1005	2	0	Give me liberty, or give me death!	0	0	2021-07-04 15:43:00.0000000	2021-07-04 15:43:00.0000000	63	10	205	4
1006	2	0	What am I doing here?	0	0	2021-07-05 17:45:00.0000000	2021-07-05 17:45:00.0000000	64	11	205	5

Calling Code

// result: both DirectChildComments & AllChildComments filled with direct child comments
// expectation: only DirectChildComments filled with direct child comments
Comment[] comments1 = await this.repoCommon.GetMulti<Comment>(x => x.PackageID == 205 && x.ParentCommentID == null, null, "DirectChildComments");

// result: both DirectChildComments & AllChildComments empty
// expectation: only AllChildComments filled with all child comments
Comment[] comments2 = await this.repoCommon.GetMulti<Comment>(x => x.PackageID == 205 && x.ParentCommentID == null, null, "AllChildComments");

// result: both DirectChildComments & AllChildComments filled with DirectChildComments
// expectation: DirectChildComments filled with direct child comments, AllChildComments filled with all child comments
Comment[] comments3 = await this.repoCommon.GetMulti<Comment>(x => x.PackageID == 205 && x.ParentCommentID == null, null, "DirectChildComments,AllChildComments");

Addendum

The test data goes 3 comment levels deep. There are 5 top level comments. Then there is 1 child comment for every top comment. Then there is 1 child comment for every child comment.

RepoCommon's string inclusion param is eventually comma-splitted and executed in an extension method as:

foreach (string include in includes)
{
	queryable = queryable.Include(include);
}

It works correctly for all other string inclusion calls. Including other entities that have multiple FKs to another entity (so not itself), where inverse properties are also defined.

Include stack traces

EF Core version: 5.08 Database provider: Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer Target framework: .NET 5.0 Operating system: Win10 IDE: Visual Studio 2019 16.10.3

item.title: Issue: Why Does Upgrading to .Net 5.0 cause error: System.InvalidOperationException: 'Nullable object must have a value.' - @PostAlmostAnything
item.description:
I just got done upgrading to .Net 5.0 and now my app returns the following error whenever I run it:

System.InvalidOperationException: 'Nullable object must have a value.'

I have not changed the queries used on the home page at all since last running the app. What changes might cause this? I do have a number of nullable types used in the query for which the values passed from the home page are null, but isn't the whole point of using nullable types so that you can pass null values?

I also get a pending database migration notice. I've never used a code first approach so I always make changes to my database using SSMS before updating the entity models by hand. Is there any way to make this notice go away other than running the migration?

item.title: Issue: EF Core 6.0.0 Preview-6 no longer compatible with .NET 5. - @Tommigun1980
item.description:
File a bug

Trying to update the EF Core packages to Preview-6 results in the following:

Checking compatibility for Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.1 with net5.0.
Package Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.1 is not compatible with net5.0 (.NETCoreApp,Version=v5.0). Package Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.1 supports: net6.0 (.NETCoreApp,Version=v6.0)
Checking compatibility for Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.1 with net5.0.
Package Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.1 is not compatible with net5.0 (.NETCoreApp,Version=v5.0). Package Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.1 supports: net6.0 (.NETCoreApp,Version=v6.0)
Checking compatibility for Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Sqlite 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.1 with net5.0.
Package Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Sqlite 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.1 is not compatible with net5.0 (.NETCoreApp,Version=v5.0). Package Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Sqlite 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.1 supports: net6.0 (.NETCoreApp,Version=v6.0)
Checking compatibility for Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Tools 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.1 with net5.0.
Package Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Tools 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.1 is not compatible with net5.0 (.NETCoreApp,Version=v5.0). Package Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Tools 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.1 supports: net6.0 (.NETCoreApp,Version=v6.0)
Checking compatibility for Spontane.MobileAppService 1.0.0 with net5.0.
Checking compatibility for Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.1 with net5.0.
Package Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.1 is not compatible with net5.0 (.NETCoreApp,Version=v5.0). Package Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.1 supports: net6.0 (.NETCoreApp,Version=v6.0)
Package restore failed. Rolling back package changes for 'MyProject.MobileAppService'.

6.0.0 Preview-5 and below packages were still compatible with .NET 5. All other ASP.NET Core 6 preview packages (like caching) are compatible with .NET 5.

Is it intentional that .NET 5 support was pulled? If so, please reconsider because it is important that we test the preview releases before release, and requiring .NET 6 support makes it not really feasible at least for me to test these packages anymore (I am using Azure App Service and don't want to go through the hoops to migrate my entire server to .NET 6 Preview). Furthermore, no other ASP.NET packages seem to require .NET 6.

If this was unintentional, could a new preview release be released very quickly as 6.0.0 Preview-6 is not really usable for a lot of us because of the .NET 6 Preview dependency.

Thank you.

Include provider and version information

EF Core version: 6.0.0-preview.6.21352.1 Database provider: Any Target framework: 5.0 Operating system: macOS Big Sur IDE: VS For Mac 8.10.6

item.title: Issue: New deferred attribute missing from BeginTransactionAsync - @mind-bending-forks
item.description:
Microsoft.Data.Sqlite version: 5.0.8 Target framework: .NET 5.0 Operating system: Linux

Support for deferred transactions was recently added (#21212), which is great, thanks. However, the deferred argument is not currently available via asynchronous calls (whereas everything else I have used so far is). So:

using ( System.Data.Common.DbTransaction transaction = sqliteConnection.BeginTransaction(deferred: true))
{
  ...

builds fine, whereas

using ( System.Data.Common.DbTransaction transaction = await sqliteConnection.BeginTransactionAsync(deferred: true))
{
  ...

reports

The best overload for 'BeginTransactionAsync' does not have a parameter named 'deferred'

or alternatively, if the deferred argument is not named, the following is reported

Argument 1: cannot convert from 'bool' to 'System.Data.IsolationLevel'

Assuming its possible to add it, support for deferred transactions via asynchronous calls too would be very welcome. Thanks.

item.title: Issue: System.InvalidOperationException: The property 'Intcol1' cannot be added to the type 'T1' because there was no property type specified and there is no corresponding CLR property or field. To add a shadow state property the property type must be specified. - @deokershesh
item.description:
In my database I have following single table with single column primary key (PK) column.

create table t1 (intcol int, charcol char(20), primary key(intcol));

Running following scaffold command results in error which says "Intcol1", note : here column name is "intcol", how/why its getting suffixed with "1"? This is causing above error (in the title). OneDB.EntityFramework.Core is provider name.

item.title: Issue: DbContextFactory: Multiple constructors accepting all given argument types have been found in type - @Schoof-T
item.description:
Ask a question

While implementing the DbContextFactory in our Blazor Server application (per described in https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/blazor/blazor-server-ef-core?view=aspnetcore-5.0) we get the exception 'Multiple constructors accepting all given argument types have been found in type'. We have a custom DbContext with multiple constructors.

Adding DbContextFactory

            services.AddDbContextFactory<ExampleDbContext>();

Custom DbContext

/// <summary>
    /// Data Access using Entity Framework
    /// </summary>
    public partial class ExampleDbContext : DbContext
    {
        private readonly IHttpContextAccessor _httpContextAccessor;

        #region construction

        public ExampleDbContext()
        {
        }

        public ExampleDbContext(IHttpContextAccessor httpContextAccessor)
        {
            _httpContextAccessor = httpContextAccessor;
        }

        public ExampleDbContext(DbContextOptions<ExampleDbContext> options, IHttpContextAccessor httpContextAccessor) : base(options)
        {
            _httpContextAccessor = httpContextAccessor;
        }

        public ExampleDbContext(DbContextOptions<ExampleDbContext> options) : base(options)
        {
        }

        #endregion

I have found the following issues regarding this: https://github.com/dotnet/efcore/issues/24124 The person in said ticket seems to have found a solution using a design time factory. We tried to implement this as well, but this did not seem to have any effect.

    public class ExampleDbContextFactory : IDesignTimeDbContextFactory<ExampleDbContext>
    {
        public ExampleDbContextDbContext CreateDbContext(string[] args)
        {
            var optionsBuilder = new DbContextOptionsBuilder<ExampleDbContext>();
            optionsBuilder.UseOracle("CONNECTIONSTRING");

            return new ExampleDbContext(optionsBuilder.Options);
        }
    }

All we had to do to implement the design time factory was just add this class in the same library as the DbContext, correct?

We noticed this issue should be fixed in the lastest daily build of .NET 6, but we cannot currently use .NET 6. Is there anything we are missing or can do in the meantime?

Include stack traces

InvalidOperationException: Multiple constructors accepting all given argument types have been found in type 'Schema.Infrastructure.ExampleDbContext'. There should only be one applicable constructor.
Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.ActivatorUtilities.TryFindMatchingConstructor(Type instanceType, Type[] argumentTypes, ref ConstructorInfo matchingConstructor, ref Nullable<int>[] parameterMap)

Include provider and version information

EF Core version: 5.0.8 Database provider: Oracle.EntityFrameworkCore Target framework: NET 5.0 Operating system: Windows 10 IDE: Visual Studio 2019 16.10

item.title: Issue: Shared column with different CLR types gets projected out twice - @roji
item.description:
In a TPH hierarchy, if two properties map to the same column, but have differing CLR types, the column gets projected twice.

Minimal repro 
await using var ctx = new BlogContext();
await ctx.Database.EnsureDeletedAsync();
await ctx.Database.EnsureCreatedAsync();

_ = ctx.Blogs.ToList();

public class BlogContext : DbContext
{
    public DbSet<Blog> Blogs { get; set; }

    static ILoggerFactory ContextLoggerFactory
        => LoggerFactory.Create(b => b.AddConsole().AddFilter("", LogLevel.Information));

    protected override void OnConfiguring(DbContextOptionsBuilder optionsBuilder)
        => optionsBuilder
            .UseSqlServer(@"Server=localhost;Database=test;User=SA;Password=Abcd5678;Connect Timeout=60;ConnectRetryCount=0")
            .EnableSensitiveDataLogging()
            .UseLoggerFactory(ContextLoggerFactory);

    protected override void OnModelCreating(ModelBuilder modelBuilder)
    {
        modelBuilder.Entity<SpecialBlog1>().Property(b => b.Foo)
            .HasColumnName("foo")
            .HasColumnType("int");
        modelBuilder.Entity<SpecialBlog2>().Property(b => b.Foo)
            .HasColumnName("foo")
            .HasColumnType("int");
    }
}

public class Blog
{
    public int Id { get; set; }
}

public class SpecialBlog1 : Blog
{
    public int Id { get; set; }
    public long Foo { get; set; }
}

public class SpecialBlog2 : Blog
{
    public int Id { get; set; }
    public short Foo { get; set; }
}

SQL:

SELECT [b].[Id], [b].[Discriminator], [b].[foo], [b].[foo]
FROM [Blogs] AS [b]

Originally flagged by @jeuxjeux20 in https://github.com/npgsql/efcore.pg/issues/1897 regarding PG JSON POCOs mapped to the same database jsonb column.

item.title: Issue: Elasticsearch provider - @YohanSciubukgian
item.description:
As document databases like CosmosDB are now supported by EF Core, could it be possible to consider having an ElasticSearch provider ?

AFAIK, EF Core relies on the CosmosDB SDK for its implementation. For Elasticsearch, NEST provides a solid baseline with attributes mapping etc that could be used to defined structures on which EF Core could relies on to request data.

item.title: Issue: Regression in 5.0.8 for Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design and Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Tools - @msawczyn
item.description:
The issue described in https://github.com/dotnet/efcore/issues/16216 is present in v5.0.8.

The workaround Bryce confirmed in that ticket is correct, and I had it working in 5.0.7 by editing PackageReference entries for Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design and Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Tools in the project file. But the update to 5.0.8 restored the problem and it had to be manually corrected again.

The TLDR recap:

    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design" Version="5.0.8">
      <PrivateAssets>all</PrivateAssets>
      <IncludeAssets>runtime; build; native; contentfiles; analyzers; buildtransitive</IncludeAssets>
    </PackageReference>

and

    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Tools" Version="5.0.8">
      <PrivateAssets>all</PrivateAssets>
      <IncludeAssets>runtime; build; native; contentfiles; analyzers; buildtransitive</IncludeAssets>
    </PackageReference>

should instead be

    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design" Version="5.0.8" />

and

    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Tools" Version="5.0.8" />

Thanks, team, for all your hard work.

item.title: Issue: AsSplitQuery + paging with ordering containing duplicates - @eh-torkaman
item.description:
File a bug

when using .Include to load some child table with .skip(n).take(n) and .AsSplitQuery() the translated query for fetching rows of child table has a subquery which is assumed to return correct set of result (same set as before) BUT imagine condition where there are more than one plausible sub set for the main query base on the exact same query (after sorting base on some fields and take top 10 rows there might be more than one possible outcome - all of the rows have same rank ) then the subquery in the .Include might return different set of results ( which happened to me - may be because of sql query optimizations ... ) of course its always possible to add order by some unique field to fix the problem but its not obvious

Possible fixes

EF by default adds sort/order by base on primary key of main table or may be the subquery in ".Include" uses foreign key of returned set

item.title: Issue: SnapshotFactoryFactory assumes wrong type for snapshot expression body - @0x0309
item.description:
Using the following value comparer results in an exception being thrown.

new ValueComparer<IEnumerable<int>>(
    (a, b) => a.SequenceEqual(b),
    a => a.Aggregate(0, (h, i) => HashCode.Combine(h, i.GetHashCode())),
    a => a.ToArray() // <--
  );

System.ArgumentException: Argument types do not match
  at System.Linq.Expressions.Expression.Condition(Expression test, Expression ifTrue, Expression ifFalse)
  at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.SnapshotFactoryFactory.CreateSnapshotValueExpression(Expression expression, IPropertyBase propertyBase)
  at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.SnapshotFactoryFactory.CreateSnapshotExpression(Type entityType, ParameterExpression parameter, Type[] types, IList`1 propertyBases)
  at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.SnapshotFactoryFactory.CreateConstructorExpression(IEntityType entityType, ParameterExpression parameter)
  at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.SnapshotFactoryFactory`1.Create(IEntityType entityType)
  at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Metadata.Internal.EntityType.<>c.<get_OriginalValuesFactory>b__184_0(EntityType entityType)
  at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Internal.NonCapturingLazyInitializer.EnsureInitialized[TParam,TValue](TValue& target, TParam param, Func`2 valueFactory)
  at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Metadata.Internal.EntityType.get_OriginalValuesFactory()
  at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.InternalEntityEntry.OriginalValues..ctor(InternalEntityEntry entry)
  at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.InternalEntityEntry.EnsureOriginalValues()
  at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.InternalEntityEntrySubscriber.SnapshotAndSubscribe(InternalEntityEntry entry)
  at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.StateManager.StartTrackingFromQuery(IEntityType baseEntityType, Object entity, ValueBuffer& valueBuffer)
  at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.QueryContext.StartTracking(IEntityType entityType, Object entity, ValueBuffer valueBuffer)
  at lambda_method45(Closure , QueryContext , DbDataReader , ResultContext , SingleQueryResultCoordinator )
  at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.Internal.SingleQueryingEnumerable`1.AsyncEnumerator.MoveNextAsync()

The bug is here

The bug is that snapshotExpression.Type is actually int[] rather than the assumed IEnumerable<int>. This is because LambdaExpression can have an implicit conversion from the type of the body expression to the delegate return type.

The logical fix would be wrapping the body expression in an explicit cast if the types don't match, like this: 1ead7ef36a9f39a572b99d2f42ec74c619b84247

Issue #11077 seems to have been caused by this same kind of bug, but the real bug was not identified there and fixed, and instead a work-around was suggested.

It is not at all clear that the return value must be explicitly cast to the exact same type as the type parameter of the ValueComparer, as this is not normally required in C# code. This should be fixed so it "just works" rather than telling people they need to cast explicitly, and hoping they manage to find that advice somewhere.

An alternative fix if this affects multiple code locations would be to fix the snapshot expression in the ValueComparer constructor so that it is automatically correct for all usages.

item.title: Issue: Improve documentation of 'dotnet ef migrations remove' for offline scenarios - @jikuja
item.description:
dotnet ef migrations add command creates new migration files without requiring connection to the database.

Otoh, if dotnet ef migrations remove is supposed to remove migration files it should work without database connection and reverting migration from the database should be handled with dotnet ef database subcommand.

Current tool version print following when adding new migrations:

Done. To undo this action, use 'ef migrations remove' that is not true if the database connection is not working.

dotnet ef migrations remove --help does not include any hint that working database connection is required or any guidance how to disable check of the applied migrations from configured database.

Possible fixes

  • Current documentation is confusing and should clarify connection requirement
  • dotnet ef migrations remove should handle only local files like add command does
  • dotnet ef migrations remove should at least have an argument to disable applied migrations checking

Stack

Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.SqlException (0x80131904): A network-related or instance-specific error occurred while establishing a connection to SQL Server. The server was not found or was not accessible. Verify that the instance name is correct and that SQL Server is configured to allow remote connections.    at Microsoft.Data.ProviderBase.DbConnectionPool.TryGetConnection(DbConnection owningObject, UInt32 waitForMultipleObjectsTimeout, Boolean allowCreate, Boolean onlyOneCheckConnection, DbConnectionOptions userOptions, DbConnectionInternal& connection)
   at Microsoft.Data.ProviderBase.DbConnectionPool.TryGetConnection(DbConnection owningObject, TaskCompletionSource`1 retry, DbConnectionOptions userOptions, DbConnectionInternal& connection)
   at Microsoft.Data.ProviderBase.DbConnectionFactory.TryGetConnection(DbConnection owningConnection, TaskCompletionSource`1 retry, DbConnectionOptions userOptions, DbConnectionInternal oldConnection, DbConnectionInternal& connection)
   at Microsoft.Data.ProviderBase.DbConnectionInternal.TryOpenConnectionInternal(DbConnection outerConnection, DbConnectionFactory connectionFactory, TaskCompletionSource`1 retry, DbConnectionOptions userOptions)
   at Microsoft.Data.ProviderBase.DbConnectionClosed.TryOpenConnection(DbConnection outerConnection, DbConnectionFactory connectionFactory, TaskCompletionSource`1 retry, DbConnectionOptions userOptions)
   at Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.SqlConnection.TryOpen(TaskCompletionSource`1 retry, SqlConnectionOverrides overrides)

   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer.Storage.Internal.SqlServerConnection.OpenDbConnection(Boolean errorsExpected)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Storage.RelationalConnection.OpenInternal(Boolean errorsExpected)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Storage.RelationalConnection.Open(Boolean errorsExpected)

   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer.Storage.Internal.SqlServerExecutionStrategy.Execute[TState,TResult](TState state, Func`3 operation, Func`3 verifySucceeded)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ExecutionStrategyExtensions.Execute[TState,TResult](IExecutionStrategy strategy, TState state, Func`2 operation, Func`2 verifySucceeded)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ExecutionStrategyExtensions.Execute[TState,TResult](IExecutionStrategy strategy, TState state, Func`2 operation)

item.title: Issue: Rewrite query in a form that can be translated, or switch to client evaluation explicitly? - @ajitgoel
item.description:
I am getting the rewrite the query in a form that can be translated error when trying to run a entity framework query with a Include and ThenInclude clause. All this code was working before the application was moved from Microsoft.EntityFramework version 3.1 to Microsoft.EntityFramework version 5.0.4.

This seems to be Uri mapping related InMemory provider bug (does not happen with SqlServer). Happens also in the latest official at this time 5.0.7. Can you please advise?

internal IEnumerable<ProductHierarchy> ProductHierarchiesWithDetail
  get
  {
    var item = ProductHierarchies.Include(x => X.BusinessAssocs).ThenInclude(x => X.Business).AsEnumerable();
    return item; 
  }
}
public class ProductHierarchy
{
  public Uri ProductHierarchyURI { get; set; }
  public IList<HierarchyBusinessAssoc> BusinessAssocs{ get; set; } = new List<HierarchyBusinessAssoc>();
}

public class Hierarchy BusinessAssoc
{
  public Uri ProducthierarchyURI { get; set; }
  public Uri BusinessURI { get; set; }
  public ProductHierarchy Hierarchy { get; set; }
  public Business Business { get; set; }
}

public class Business
{
  public Uri BusinessURI { get; set; }
  public IList<HierarchyBusinessAssoc> HierarchyAssocs { get; set; } = new List<HierarchyBusinessAssoc>();
  public IList<ProductType> ProductTypes { get; set; } = new List<ProductType>();
}

public class ProductHierarchyEntityConfiguration : IEntityTypeConfiguration<ProductHierarchy>
{
public void configure(EntityTypeBuilder<ProductHierarchy> builder)
{
  builder. Totable("ProductHierarchy");
  builder.Haskey(e => new { e.ProducthierarchyURI });
  builder.HasMany(x => x.BusinessAssocs).WithOne (assoc => assoc.Hierarchy);
}

{
  public void Configure(EntityTypeBuilder<HierarchyBusinessAssoc> builder)
  {
    builder.ToTable("ProductHieracyBusinessAssociation");
    builder.Haskey(e => new { e.producthierarchyURI, e.BusinessURI });
    builder.Hasone (assoc => assoc.Hierarchy).WithMany (h => h.BusinessAssocs).HasForeignKey(assoc => assoc.ProductHierarchyURI);
    builder.HasOne (assoc => assoc. Business).WithMany (ph => ph.HierarchyAssocs).HasForeignKey(x => x.BusinessURI);
  }
}

public class BusinessEntityConfiguration : IEntityTypeConfiguration<Business>
{
  public void Configure(EntityTypeBuilder<Business> builder)
  {
    builder.ToTable("Business");
    builder.Haskey(e => new {e.BusinessURI});
    builder.HasMany (pt => pt.HierarchyAssocs).WithOne(x => x.Business).HasForeignKey(x => x.BusinessURI);
  }
}

Exception:

ProjectionBindingExpression: EmptyProjection MemberIsNullable: False
,"ProductHierarchyURI") != null && object.Equals(objA: EF.Property<Uri> (EntityShaperExpression:
EntityType: ProductHierarchyValueBufferExpression:ProjectionBindingExpression: Empty Projection MemberIsNullable: False,"ProductHierarchyURI"), objB: EF.Property<Uri>(h, "ProductHierarchyURI")))
Join
inner: DbSet<Business> 0.outerKeySelector: h => EF.Property<Uri>(h, "BusinessURI"),
innerKeySelector: b => EF.Property<Uri>(b, "BusinessURI"),
resultSelector: (0, 1) => new TransparentIdentifier<HierarchyBusinessAssoc, Business>
Outer = 0,Inner = i' could not be translated. Either rewrite the query in a form that can be translated, or switch to client evaluation explicitly by inserting a call to 'AsEnumerable', 'AsAsyncEnumerable', 'ToList', or 'ToListAsync'.

item.title: Issue: [EF Core Cosmos] Translation of method 'string.Contains' with comparisonType failed. - @aguang-xyz
item.description:

The CONTAINS function of Cosmos DB supports case-insensitive searching. So I'm assuming that in EF Core with Cosmos Provider, we should be able to use string.contains with comparisionType specified. But when I try to do that, I got a translation failure.

My code

var keyword = "xxx";
var count = _dbContext<ProductDAO>()
                      .Where(product => product.Name.Contains(keyword, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                      .Count();

item.title: Issue: concatenating two diffrent entity queries that mapped to same viewmodel throws error - @metrocoding
item.description:
Hi, I have a problem with concatenating two queries. I have two different models I query them from the database and mapping them to the same ViewModel using auto mapper ProjectTo(). i can get result when I'm calling ToListAsync() on each query but when I concat two queries I face this error:

System.InvalidOperationException: Different projection mapping count in set operation at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Query.SqlExpressions.SelectExpression.ApplySetOperation(SetOperationType setOperationType, SelectExpression select2, Boolean distinct)...

my TOff model

public class TOff : ITOff

Line Model

        public int LastEditorUserId { get; set; }

        [ForeignKey("Department")]
        public int DepartmentId { get; set; }

        public virtual User LastEditorUser { get; set; }

my view model

        public int? LineId { get; set; }

        public virtual string DispatchingCode { get; set; }

        [Required]
        public int DepartmentId { get; set; }

        [StringLength(256)]
        public string Description { get; set; }
    }

my BL code

var lineQuery = _lineRepository.DeferredWhere(l => !l.IsDeleted).ProjectTo<ListLineViewModel>(
                    new MapperConfiguration(cfg => cfg.CreateMap<Line, ListLineViewModel>()
                        .ForMember(line => line.EndPostName, line => line.MapFrom(l => l.EndPostBay.Name))

));

                        .ForMember(line => line.StartPostBayId, line => line.MapFrom(l => 0))
                        .ForMember(line => line.EndPostBayId, line => line.MapFrom(l => l.PostBay.Id))
                        .ForMember(line => line.TOffName, line =>  line.MapFrom(l => l.Line.Name))
));

var toffList = await tOffQuery.ToListAsync();
var lineList = await lineQuery.ToListAsync();

var lineListQuery = lineQuery.Concat(tOffQuery);

// line that throw error

is there any way to make this work properly?

item.title: Issue: Are you using (and how do you use) the Entity Framework Designer? - @JeremyLikness
item.description:
Do you and/or your team use the Entity Framework designer on a regular basis? The designer is the editor for EDMX files that is built right into Visual Studio. The EF Core team would like to understand:

  • How you are using the designer today, i.e. just viewing models vs. customizing each release.
  • If you have plans to migrate to EF Core and how we can help.
  • What obstacles you may be facing.
  • If you are aware of visualization alternatives like EF Core Power Tools or Entity Framework Visual Designer.

Please feel free to leave details in the comments for this issue, or reach out to me to set up time for a quick chat.

Thank you!

item.title: Issue: Query/Test: optimize data seeding for temporal query tests - @maumar
item.description: Currently we start with regular data seeding, then remove entities using EF and then fixup history table. We could consider using sql directly so it's faster, also perform renames rather than deletes, so we don't need to worry about constraints when deleting the entities.

item.title: Issue: "Invalid column name" when saving, but the columns were removed several migrations ago - @MrYossu
item.description:
I have an ASP.NET Core 3.1 Razor Pages site, using EF Core 3.1.8 against SQL Server.

One of the models is named Global, and is used to store user-configurable site settings. There is only one row in this table.

The code that modifies this looks like this...

    public async Task OnPost(Global global) {
      Global g = await _appDbContext.Globals.SingleAsync();
      // Modify the properties on g
      await _appDbContext.SaveChangesAsync();
      Response.Redirect("/Office");
    }

Up until a week or so ago, the Global model had these two properties...

    [Column(TypeName = "decimal(18,2)")]
    public decimal DeliveryCharge { get; set; }
    [Column(TypeName = "decimal(18,2)")]
    public decimal FreeDeliveryOver { get; set; }

However, these were removed, and a new model added that included these two properties and some others. The database has been updated with three migrations snice then, and we haven't seen any problems.

Yesterday, I did another update (database migration and code update), and now when the Global is updated (see code above), we get an exception...

Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Update - An exception occurred in the database while saving changes for context type 'MyWebSite.Models.AppDbContext'.
Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbUpdateException: An error occurred while updating the entries. See the inner exception for details.
 ---> Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.SqlException (0x80131904): Invalid column name 'DeliveryCharge'.
   at Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.SqlConnection.OnError(SqlException exception, Boolean breakConnection, Action`1 wrapCloseInAction)
   at Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.SqlInternalConnection.OnError(SqlException exception, Boolean breakConnection, Action`1 wrapCloseInAction)
   at Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.TdsParser.ThrowExceptionAndWarning(TdsParserStateObject stateObj, Boolean callerHasConnectionLock, Boolean asyncClose)
   at Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.TdsParser.TryRun(RunBehavior runBehavior, SqlCommand cmdHandler, SqlDataReader dataStream, BulkCopySimpleResultSet bulkCopyHandler, TdsParserStateObject stateObj, Boolean& dataReady)
   at Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.SqlDataReader.TryHasMoreRows(Boolean& moreRows)
   at Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.SqlDataReader.TryHasMoreResults(Boolean& moreResults)
   at Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.SqlDataReader.TryNextResult(Boolean& more)
   at Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.SqlDataReader.<>c__DisplayClass193_0.<NextResultAsync>b__1(Task t)
   at Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.SqlDataReader.InvokeRetryable[T](Func`2 moreFunc, TaskCompletionSource`1 source, IDisposable objectToDispose)
--- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown ---
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Update.AffectedCountModificationCommandBatch.ConsumeAsync(RelationalDataReader reader, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
ClientConnectionId:6b7267ea-2786-43db-85ef-3ccda25b305f
Error Number:207,State:1,Class:16
   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Update.AffectedCountModificationCommandBatch.ConsumeAsync(RelationalDataReader reader, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Update.ReaderModificationCommandBatch.ExecuteAsync(IRelationalConnection connection, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Update.ReaderModificationCommandBatch.ExecuteAsync(IRelationalConnection connection, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Update.Internal.BatchExecutor.ExecuteAsync(IEnumerable`1 commandBatches, IRelationalConnection connection, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Update.Internal.BatchExecutor.ExecuteAsync(IEnumerable`1 commandBatches, IRelationalConnection connection, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.StateManager.SaveChangesAsync(IList`1 entriesToSave, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.StateManager.SaveChangesAsync(DbContext _, Boolean acceptAllChangesOnSuccess, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer.Storage.Internal.SqlServerExecutionStrategy.ExecuteAsync[TState,TResult](TState state, Func`4 operation, Func`4 verifySucceeded, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext.SaveChangesAsync(Boolean acceptAllChangesOnSuccess, CancellationToken cancellationToken)

I have checked and double-checked, and the code does not contain any reference to DeliveryCharge anywhere (other than in the migration files where it was added, and subsequently removed), so I have no idea why EF thinks it should be there. If I add the DeliveryCharge property back in, then I get the same exception on the (missing) FreeDeliveryOver property.

The code works fine when run locally, and also against the test database. However, if I change the connection string of my local code (in Visual Studio) to point at the live database, I get the same exception. This is all using the same code. I've re-applied the migration and restarted the site, but nothing helped.

If I add the two properties back to Global and update, then the code runs fine on the live site. However, I don't want to leave them in, as they shouldn't be there. More to the point, I would like to know why this exception is being raised, when the code doesn't have any reference to them.

Thanks for any help you can give.

EF Core version: 3.1.8 Database provider: Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer Target framework: .NET 3.1 Operating system: Locally Windows 10, latest updates. The server is running latest version of Windows Server (not sure exactly) IDE: Visual Studio 2019 16.10

item.title: Issue: System.Linq.Queryable.TakeLast() could not be translated. - @DariuszGit
item.description:
Hello,

I have the following code:

Context.ApplicationVersions.OrderBy(x => x.ReleaseDate).TakeLast(versionsToRemoveCount).ToList();

The code throws an exception:

System.InvalidOperationException: 'The LINQ expression 'DbSet() .OrderBy(x => x.ReleaseDate) .TakeLast(__p_0)' could not be translated. Either rewrite the query in a form that can be translated, or switch to client evaluation explicitly by inserting a call to 'AsEnumerable', 'AsAsyncEnumerable', 'ToList', or 'ToListAsync'.

When I change the code to:

Context.ApplicationVersions.OrderByDescending(x => x.ReleaseDate).Take(versionsToRemoveCount).ToList();

everything works fine.

Data info: ReleaseDate is type of DateTime, versionsToRemoveCount is type of Int32.

I am not sure if this behavior is correct or it is a bug. Please take a look into it. Thank you!

EF Core version: 5.0.2 Database provider: Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer Target framework: .NET 5.0 Operating system: Windows 10 IDE: Visual Studio 2019 16.10.3

item.title: Issue: One to many relationship > Mandatory FK with DeleteBehaviour Restricted > does not allow removing child entities - @maulik-modi
item.description:
We are trying to use DDD style repository pattern per Aggregate root but unable to delete child entities with FK association: DeleteBehavior Restricted. Please find attached sample with Blog and Post.

Constraint We are not allowed to use Delete Behaviour: Cascade in EF Core or Database as both may lead to accidental data loss.

Include your code

 public class Post
    {
        public int Id { get; set; }
        public int BlogId { get; set; }
        public Blog Blog { get; set; }
    }

public class Blog
    {
        public int Id { get; set; }
        public ICollection<Post> Posts { get; set; }
    }

 public class BlogRepository : IBlog
    {
        private readonly BlogContext _applicationdbContext;

        public BlogRepository(BlogContext dbContext)
        {
            _applicationdbContext = dbContext;
        }

        public Blog Get(int id)
        {
            var blog = _applicationdbContext.Blogs
                        .Where(s => s.Id == id)
                        .AsSplitQuery()
                        .Include(s => s.Posts)
                        .FirstOrDefault();

            return blog;
        }

  public void SaveChanges()
        {
            _applicationdbContext.SaveChanges();
        }
}

 public class Program
    {

        public static void Main()
        {
            var collection = new ServiceCollection();
            collection
                .AddDbContext<BlogContext>()
                .AddScoped<IBlog, BlogRepository>();

            var service = collection.BuildServiceProvider();
            var _repo = service.GetService<IBlog>();

            //Fetching Record
            var blog = _repo.Get(1);

            //Updating BlogID with existing Post
            var data = blog.Posts.FirstOrDefault();
            if (data != null)
                data.BlogId = 2;

            ///Removing post from entity
            int postId = data.Id;
            var removeData = blog.Posts.FirstOrDefault(x => x.Id != postId);
            if (removeData != null)
                blog.Posts.Remove(removeData);

            // Adding New Post with blog
            var post = new Post();
            blog.Posts.Add(post);
       
            _repo.SaveChanges();
        }
    }

 public class PostConfig : IEntityTypeConfiguration<Post>
    {
        public void Configure(EntityTypeBuilder<Post> builder)
        {
            builder.ToTable("post");
            builder.HasKey(t => t.Id);
            builder.HasOne(d => d.Blog)
            .WithMany(p => p.Posts)
            .HasForeignKey(d => d.BlogId)
            .OnDelete(DeleteBehavior.Restrict)
            .HasConstraintName("FK_Blog_Id_Post_BlogID");
        }
    }

  public class BlogConfig : IEntityTypeConfiguration<Blog>
    {
        public void Configure(EntityTypeBuilder<Blog> builder)
        {
            builder.ToTable("blog");
            builder.HasKey(t => t.Id);
        }
    }

 public class BlogContext : DbContext
    {
        public DbSet<Blog> Blogs { get; set; }
        public DbSet<Post> Posts { get; set; }

        protected override void OnConfiguring(DbContextOptionsBuilder optionsBuilder)
        {
            optionsBuilder.UseNpgsql(
                "Server=localhost;port=5432;user id=postgres;password=password;database=SampleDB;pooling=true;");
        }

        protected override void OnModelCreating(ModelBuilder modelBuilder)
        {
            modelBuilder.ApplyConfiguration(new BlogConfig());

            modelBuilder.ApplyConfiguration(new PostConfig());
        }
    }

RestrictDelete.zip

Include stack traces

              Error:The association between entity types 'Blog' and 'Post' has been severed, 
            but the relationship is either marked as required or is implicitly required because the foreign key is not nullable.
 
            If the dependent/ child entity should be deleted when a required relationship is severed, 
            configure the relationship to use cascade deletes. Consider using 'DbContextOptionsBuilder.
             EnableSensitiveDataLogging' to see the key values.'

   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.InternalEntityEntry.HandleConceptualNulls(Boolean sensitiveLoggingEnabled, Boolean force, Boolean isCascadeDelete)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.InternalEntityEntry.SetProperty(IPropertyBase propertyBase, Object value, Boolean isMaterialization, Boolean setModified, Boolean isCascadeDelete, CurrentValueType valueType)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.InternalEntityEntry.SetProperty(IPropertyBase propertyBase, Object value, Boolean isMaterialization, Boolean setModified, Boolean isCascadeDelete)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.NavigationFixer.ConditionallyNullForeignKeyProperties(InternalEntityEntry dependentEntry, InternalEntityEntry principalEntry, IForeignKey foreignKey)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.NavigationFixer.NavigationCollectionChanged(InternalEntityEntry entry, INavigationBase navigationBase, IEnumerable`1 added, IEnumerable`1 removed)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.InternalEntityEntryNotifier.NavigationCollectionChanged(InternalEntityEntry entry, INavigationBase navigationBase, IEnumerable`1 added, IEnumerable`1 removed)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.ChangeDetector.DetectNavigationChange(InternalEntityEntry entry, INavigationBase navigationBase)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.ChangeDetector.LocalDetectChanges(InternalEntityEntry entry)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.ChangeDetector.DetectChanges(IStateManager stateManager)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.ChangeTracker.DetectChanges()
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext.TryDetectChanges()
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext.SaveChanges(Boolean acceptAllChangesOnSuccess)
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext.SaveChanges()
   at CascadeDelete.BlogRepository.SaveChanges() in D:\Projects\POC\CascadeDelete\CascadeDelete\BlogRepository.cs:line 40
   at CascadeDelete.Program.Main() in D:\Projects\POC\CascadeDelete\CascadeDelete\Program.cs:line 51
